message.content:
Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Make `evaluate` the default CLI subcommand

## Context

Running `./dist/bin/context-evaluator-darwin-arm64 cli --url <url>` fails with `error: unknown option '--url'` because `--url` is defined on the `evaluate` subcommand, not the root `cli` program. Users must currently type the full `cli evaluate --url <url>`, but since `evaluate` is the **only** subcommand, it should be the default.

## Approach

Make `evaluate` the default command in Commander.js so that `cli --url <url>` is equivalent to `cli evaluate --url <url>`.

### File to modify

**`src/cli/index.ts`** (line 25)

Add `.default()` to the evaluate command definition:

```typescript
program
  .command("evaluate", { isDefault: true })  // ← add { isDefault: true }
  .description(...)
```

This is a single-line change. Commander.js's `isDefault` option makes the command run when no subcommand is matched, so all existing `--url`, `--path`, etc. options will work directly after `cli`.

## Verification

1. Run `bun run test` to ensure no regressions
2. Test both invocations work:
   - `bun run src/index.ts cli evaluate --url https://github.com/jayvicsanantonio/ai-timeline-automation` (existing)
   - `bun run src/index.ts cli --url https://github.com/jayvicsanantonio/ai-timeline-automation` (new)
3. Verify `cli --help` still shows the evaluate subcommand
4. Run `bun run lint`


If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/cedricteyton/.REDACTED.jsonl

planContent:
# Plan: Make `evaluate` the default CLI subcommand

## Context

Running `./dist/bin/context-evaluator-darwin-arm64 cli --url <url>` fails with `error: unknown option '--url'` because `--url` is defined on the `evaluate` subcommand, not the root `cli` program. Users must currently type the full `cli evaluate --url <url>`, but since `evaluate` is the **only** subcommand, it should be the default.

## Approach

Make `evaluate` the default command in Commander.js so that `cli --url <url>` is equivalent to `cli evaluate --url <url>`.

### File to modify

**`src/cli/index.ts`** (line 25)

Add `.default()` to the evaluate command definition:

```typescript
program
  .command("evaluate", { isDefault: true })  // ← add { isDefault: true }
  .description(...)
```

This is a single-line change. Commander.js's `isDefault` option makes the command run when no subcommand is matched, so all existing `--url`, `--path`, etc. options will work directly after `cli`.

## Verification

1. Run `bun run test` to ensure no regressions
2. Test both invocations work:
   - `bun run src/index.ts cli evaluate --url https://github.com/jayvicsanantonio/ai-timeline-automation` (existing)
   - `bun run src/index.ts cli --url https://github.com/jayvicsanantonio/ai-timeline-automation` (new)
3. Verify `cli --help` still shows the evaluate subcommand
4. Run `bun run lint`

content.text: Let me read the file and make the change.

content.text: All 1351 tests pass and lint is clean. The change is complete — added `{ isDefault: true }` to the `evaluate` command in `src/cli/index.ts:25`, so `cli --url <url>` now works the same as `cli evaluate --url <url>`.

content.content:
src/cli/index.ts | 28 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
 1 file changed, 27 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

--- Changes ---

📄 src/cli/index.ts
  @@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
  -	.command("evaluate")
  +	.command("evaluate", { isDefault: true })
   	.description(
   		"Evaluate all AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and copilot-instructions.md files in the current directory or a remote repository",
   	)
  @@ -149,6 +149,32 @@
  +		// Auto-detect local paths passed to --url and redirect to --path
  +		if (options.url && !options.path) {
  +			const urlValue = options.url;
  +			const looksLikeLocalPath =
  +				urlValue === "." ||
  +				urlValue.startsWith("/") ||
  +				urlValue.startsWith("./") ||
  +				urlValue.startsWith("../") ||
  +				urlValue.startsWith("~");
  +			const looksLikeUrl = urlValue.includes("://");
  ... (truncated)
  +27 -1

input.command:
git add src/cli/index.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Make evaluate the default CLI subcommand

Allow `cli --url <url>` to work without explicitly typing `cli evaluate --url <url>`, since evaluate is the only subcommand.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
